feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Datasphere
feed.subtitle: SAP Datasphere Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SFTP to SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
I understand in SAP Datasphere (SAP BDC) Trail version we can not create SFTP connection. How ever I tried to prepare steps in sequence as prerequisites to create SFTP --> SAP Datasphere . Hope this will be helpful for understanding purpose.

I request community members, to let me know if I have any mistakes in below steps and if there is any way to create SFTP --> SAP Datasphere in trail version.

From your Laptop Start Menu à Operational features

 

 

Check OpenSSH Client might already available

 

To install SSH Server click on Add a features

In search bar type Open --> OpenSSH Server --> Install

 

 

Goto Control Panel --> Click on Windows Defender Firewall --> Advanced Settings --> Inbound rules --> New Role

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

From search bar search for Services

Try to find OpenSSH Server --> Right Click Properties

General Tab --> Startup Type = Automatic and click on Start        

Apply , OK and close window

 

 

 

Install WinSCP

From Google download WinSCP for windows --> go for official website

Run the .exe file for all users

Go with Default installation (Typical installation, Comander , install)

Select only check box Launch WinSCP --> Finish

As it will launch WinSCP

File Protocol = SFTP

 

SAP Cloud Connector

Log in SAP Cloud Connector: https://localhost:8443/

Add Subaccount

Goto Cloud and you can leave Host, Port, User and Password as blank

Click on Next

 

Configure Manually

 

 

 

 

In trail Version you can select Region as US East (VA) – AWS only. Other wise you can select any region. I copied Region and Subaccount from SAP BTP Cockpit as shown below screen shot. Normally in paid version you can select as per license

Login E-Mail/ID – This is of SAP BTP and Password also BTP.

 

SAP BTP Cockpit

 

 

Log on to SAP Cloud Connector à Select Subaccount crated in SAP Cloud Connector

 

 

 

 

 

SAP Cloud Connector à SFTP

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

How to get Host Key

Before Going to SAP Datasphere,

Goto Windows Power Shell and type below syntax

ssh-keyscan -p 22 sftp.XXX.XXX.X.X. hostkey.txt (in place of XXX.XXX.X.X – give your IP address)

Then it will open a notepad with Key.

But you need to take the last line in genearted Host Key from the note pad as like below

ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIB+Q5+GDouuDgLtJqjaGxCAAnuCTcyM3q2NuZKJ24zuf

 

SAP Datasphere à Connections

 

 

 

 

 

Enter Host Key and give WinSCP Host details (SFTP)

ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAIB+Q5+GDouuDgLtJqjaGxCAAnuCTcyM3q2NuZKJ24zuf

 

 

 

We get below error in Free Trail Version

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Missing Steps

In SAP Datasphere (BDC) Trail version we don’t have access to System à Administration

 

 

 

If it gets success

Datasphere → BTP → Cloud Connector → SFTP is working.

entry.title: No possibility to register FileAdapter in DPAgent 2.8.0 with Datasphere
entry.content:
I have installed and connected DPAgent 2.8.0 with Datasphere. The status is on green in Datasphere and I can register the available Adapters (for instance, ABAP). As it is indicated in note 3487646, it is not indicating the connection to HANA in the Agent with versions over 2.7.4. 

In the list of available Adapters in Datasphere, it is not shown FileAdapter as an option. Then, I try to register it with CLI configuration tool agentcli.bat --configAgent, but the next message appears:

Can anyone help me registering FileAdapter, please?

entry.title: Datasphere AWS S3 Connection to a DTP Object Store Service not possible
entry.content:
I have an AWS S3 Object Store created on our BTP Account. A Service Key has been created. When I try to validate the connection in Datasphere the next message appears:

Cause: Possible causes of error: Detailed error message (user: arn:aws:iam::'user'  is not authorized to perform: s3:listallmybuckets because no identity\-based policy allows the s3:listallmybuckets action (service: amazon s3; status code: 403; error code: accessdenied; request id: 8bg3wzykpdqw7p1m; s3 extended request id: 'id'.

Seeing this message, I want to try to afford the corresponding policies to the user of the Object Store (as indicated in SAP Datasphere - How to configure the AWS Assume Role option for an AWS S3 connection).

Anyone knows how to do it for a BTP Object Store or is there a limitation?

entry.title: Create space button is not available in free trail of SAP Dataspere.
entry.content:
Hi,
I have created a SAP Datasphere trail version and I tried to create Sapce in Datasphere, Create button is not available. I think the trail version i am using might be 30 days one or it would not be advaced trail version. If any other issue please advice me to fix it or if the issue is with trail version please share 90 days trail link or advice me how to fix it.

entry.title: Minimum Roles and Authorization for Technical user for Datasphere Prod Connection
entry.content: We are configuring technical user for Datasphere Prod connection with S4H on premise system and looking for minimum Roles and Authorization for this Technical user relevant to our SAP systems. We cannot provide SAP_ALL access in production. Please suggest SAP Note or KBA.

entry.title: SAP datasphere task chain: sql procedure not visible
entry.content:
I've created an SQL procedure in my Open SQL Schema of SPACE  "SAP_ADMIN.

I'd like to run this procedure in a Task Chain in SAP DSP, in the SPACE_ID = SAP_ADMIN. I'm following this: Run Open SQL Procedures in a Task Chain | SAP Help Portal 

However, even after assigning the privileges to the space as described here: Allow the Space to Access the Open SQL Schema | SAP Help Portal , I'm not able to see the SQL procedure in during Task Chain modelling.

The error showed is: No procedures have been created or the EXECUTE privilege has not been granted to the Open SQL schema. 

Could you please investigate?

entry.title: How to create a report of manage sales prices in SAC using Datasphere model ?
entry.content:
Hi, Actually there is a requirement where I need to create a report in SAC using Datasphere model which shows the details of 'manage sales prices' fiori app of S4HANA Public cloud Edition. Report should show all the Standard prices of Products and also if there is some changes in the prices it show all the change logs along with the user details who did the changes and with the current condition amount. I created a custom CDS in FIORI by utilizing a standard CDS I_SLSPRCGCONDITIONRECORD but this is not holding Product details.

SAP Datasphere  SAP Analytics Cloud  SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition  SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales

entry.title: How to get the internal UUID of a story calculated measure in SAC to be used in addMeasure(<UUID>) ?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am working in SAP Analytics Cloud with a chart based on a Datasphere live model.

I need to add a calculated measure (running totals) dynamically in an onSelect script. The scripting works when I use a UUID/GUID for some existing calculated measures that were created before by another developer.

Example:

Chart_1.addMeasure("92686182-6103-4765-8764-242384601111");

This works for some old calculated measures, so SAC is clearly accepting an internal UUID for those measures.

My problem is that for a new calculated measure I created, I cannot find the UUID.

What I tried:

  • Chart_1.getDataSource().getMeasures()
  • logging the full data source measures
  • trying the measure description
  • trying the user-defined measure ID

But getMeasures() only returns the backend/model measures from Datasphere, and my calculated measure is not included there.

So my questions are:

  1. In SAC scripting, how can I retrieve the internal UUID of a calculated measure created in the story?
  2. Is there any supported API to access story calculated measures for a chart based on a Datasphere live model?

Any guidance or best practice would be appreciated.

Thanks.

entry.title: SAP Datasphere and Power bi Connection by SAP HANA Database connector
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 

We want to connect our datasphere with Power bi by SAP HANA DATABASE.

i tried in Power BI Desktop i given all the details in SAP HANA DATABASE connector but still it doest show any thing.

 

Let us know who we can connect it and it will show the data.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Issues with CDS based Delta Extraction in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello,

I have got some warning and failed messages during Delta load execution.

  1. Error Occurred During API execution Activity
  2. Subscription Interface error
  3. Error while processing subscription of CDS view
  4. Error while reading reconstruction metadata for CDS view xxxxx
  5. CDS view xxxxx is inconsistent
  6. Definition of view is too complex for automatic CDC [warning]

I have not yet released the C1 contract.
If I proceed with the release, will it resolve the issues mentioned above?
Or are there additional changes required from my side?

Also I am pasting the annotations which I have used

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
@EndUserText.label: 'Interface View for zzxxxxx'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@analytics.dataExtraction.enabled: true
@analytics.dataExtraction.delta.changeDataCapture.automatic: true

entry.title: SAP DSP: persist API TASK payload in a local table
entry.content:
Dear SAP colleagues,

I'm experimenting the API task option in SAP DSP Task Chain.

I've configured correctly my HTTP with the proper authentication and I'm able to retrieve data from a specific internal and private API.

However, my objective would be persist those data in a physical table so that I can integrate those data into my data domain.

I've tried to create an SQL Script Procedure on the Open SQL Schema, with Input Parameter a string (that should be my payload) that is inserting the data into a DB table.

 I'm not able to pass the API Response of the first task to the SQL Procedure as input parameter.

Do you know if there's any possibility to do that or I'm doing something totally insane?

Thanks and regards,

i

entry.title: Passing dynamic values to Input parameters for CDS view for Data extraction from Datashere
entry.content:
Hello All,

We are looking for a solution to pass the dynamic values to the Input Parameters of the CDS view C_InvtryTurnoverAnalysis for the data extraction to Datasphere. We are using this SAP-provided CDS view C_InvtryTurnoverAnalysis in a custom CDS view and passing the static values for the dates currently to extract the Inventory Turnover every month. We did try passing the Date Functions to the Input parameters and getting the syntax errors. SAP Datasphere also has not provided the feature yet to extract the data from CDS views that have Input Parameters.

Below is the Date functions used to pass the dynamic previous month value to the Input parameter in the Custom CDS view which is using C_InvtryTurnoverAnalysis and throwing the syntax error.

P_StartDate : cast(
concat(
substring(
cast( add_months( $session.system_date, -1 ) as abap.char(8) ),
1,
6
),
'01'
)
as abap.dats
)

Following is the date functions tried to pass to the input parameters and getting the syntax errors.

P_StartDate : cast( concat( substring( cast( DATS_ADD_MONTHS( $session.system_date, -1) as abap.char(8) ), 1, 6 ), '01') as abap.dats ),

P_EndDate : DATS_ADD_DAYS(DATS_ADD_MONTHS(cast( concat( substring( cast( DATS_ADD_MONTHS( $session.system_date, -1 ) as abap.char(8) ), 1, 6 ), '01') as abap.dats ),1),-1)

Appreciate if you have come across the similar scenario and will help if any recommendations.

SAP Datasphere ABAP Development SAP BTP ABAP environment

entry.title: [SAC Trial] Authorization Request Error when connecting to SAP Datasphere (OData Connection)
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I am currently trying to create an OData connection from SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC Trial – AP11) to SAP Datasphere.

However, I am facing an issue during the OAuth authentication step.

Environment:

  • SAC URL: https://academy-t-sac.ap11.hcs.clod.sap
  • Datasphere URL: https://academydatasphere-1.ap11.hcs.cloud.sap

Connection Configuration:

  • Authentication Type: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code
  • Data Service URL:
    https://academydatasphere-1.ap11.hcs.cloud.sap/api/v1/dwc/consumption/analytical/SPACE_00005323/Analytic_Model_GE298598
  • Token URL:
    https://academydatasphere-1.authentication.ap11.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token
  • Authorization URL:
    https://academydatasphere-1.authentication.ap11.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/authorize
  • OAuth Client ID: (from SAP Learning guide)
  • Secret: (from SAP Learning guide)

Issue:
When I click "Create", SAC redirects to the authorization URL but shows the following error:

What I have tried:

  • Switched endpoints from academydatasphere-1 to academy
  • Verified Space ID (SPACE_00005323)
  • Checked Data Service URL format
  • Tested authorization endpoint directly in browser

Questions:

  1. Are the academydatasphere-1 endpoints still valid for AP11 trial?
  2. Has the OAuth client (from SAP Learning material) expired or changed?
  3. Is there any required redirect URI configuration for SAC trial connections?
  4. What is the correct configuration for current SAP Datasphere + SAC trial landscape?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated. Thank you!

entry.title: Why is my Valid OData Connection not visible in Connections in DataSphere DataFlow
entry.content:
Hello,

I've defined a valid OData connection from SAP DS towards our MS Graph https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/. with a Oath2.0 authorization.

When testing the connection with Validate this is OK, but whe I look into Connections in a DataFlow it doesn't appear ..

Any suggestions ?

entry.title: Does Sap datasphere supports transferring wirting outbound data to snowflake table
entry.content:
We are evaluating SAP Datasphere / SAP Business Data Cloud capabilities for data integration with Snowflake.

From the SAP documentation, we understand that Snowflake is supported in SAP Datasphere via Open Connectors. However, the documentation appears to describe Open Connectors primarily as inbound data sources into Datasphere, i.e., for reading or ingesting data.

Clarification requested

Can SAP Datasphere natively support writing or loading data into Snowflake tables, for example:

  • Persisting data from Datasphere into Snowflake tables
  • Supporting batch or scheduled loads
  • Acting as an outbound integration target for Snowflake

If this is supported:

  • What SAP‑recommended mechanism enables this?

If this is not supported:

  • Is Datasphere intended to be consumption‑only with respect to Snowflake, with outbound persistence handled by other SAP services?

We are looking for product‑level clarification on SAP Datasphere capabilities (not solution design or consulting guidance).

Any official clarification or experience‑based insights would be appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Datasphereにてアルファ変換を実施する
entry.content:
S/4のアルファ変換のような動作を行いたいのですがDatasphereにアルファ変換は機能として実現できますでしょうか？

実現したいことはDatasphereのローカルテーブルにCSVファイルでアップロードされたデータに対して前ゼロ無しでアップロードされたデータに対して特定の項目、特定の条件の場合に前ゼロ有にするような処理をすることを目的としています。

entry.title: Cannot Connect to SAP Datasphere Space by ODBC, asking provide X509 certificate
entry.content:
I am trying to connect to my SAP Datasphere Space database via ODBC, and I am getting the following error: "Provide X509 certificate file." Could someone let me know where I can get the file it's asking for? Or If anyone can tell me if we can disable this option of asking certificate and use only username and password, then it will be ok

 

**Note: This error is coming when i connect from Alteryx an ETL tool.**

**Update 1: I checked the odbc connection from my system and it is showing connection succesful both in windows 64-bit ODBC manager and also in Alteryx ETL, but when I query from the Alteryx Input tool it throws the same error above**


**WINDOWS 64-bit ODBC STATUS**

 

**Alteryx Data Connection Manager (DCM) STATUS**

 

**WHEN USE INPUT TOOL FOR THE DCM IN ALTERYX SHOWING ERROR**

entry.title: DataSphere OData Connection Paging
entry.content:
Hello,

I've managed tocreate a OData connection from DS to MSGraph, loading our AAD users and so on would be the goal.

The connection is working for remote tables, I can see the tables and inport them, but when fetching the data I'm always getting this error.

internal error: DPAdapterResultSet::Fetch:connection to graph.microsoft.com:443 returned status 400 message: {"error":{"code":"Request_UnsupportedQuery","message":"Invalid page size specified: '1000'. Must be between 1 and 999 inclusive.","innerError":{"date":"2026-04-09T07:55:56","request-id":"ead338c2-xxxx-481f-xxxx-2b5321c4c2de","client-request-id":"ead338c2-xxxx-481f-xxxx-2b5321c4c2de"}}}

Iv' specified in the ODataconnection extra headers

But somehow this is being ignored, anybody has an idea what's wrong ?

entry.title: SAP DataSphere – Moving Objects: Table, Dataflow from one Space to another Space.
entry.content:
During a recent implementation, an observation was made while moving objects between spaces in   After transporting a view from one space to another, field names were automatically converted to uppercase.

This post highlights the reason behind this behavior and its impact.

⸻

What Happened?
• A view was moved from a source space to a target space
• After deployment in the target space, all field names appeared in uppercase
• Previously, the field names were in mixed case (e.g., AssetNumber)

⸻

Why Does This Happen?


• Objects are not directly copied between spaces
• They are exported and re-imported into the target space
• During this process, objects are re-created and deployed again
• Metadata is regenerated, and system standardization is applied
• This results in technical field names being converted to uppercase

entry.title: How to Connect HR Non SAP System to BDC Data Sphere to See the Analytics Model and Views in SAC
entry.content:
Hi  Team,

How to Connect HR Non-SAP System to BDC Data Sphere is a client requirement. Would you kindly assist in providing the necessary documents Links and blogs for the same?  It would be beneficial to work on the Project.

Thanks & Regards,
 BASIS SUPPORT.
